verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When Jesus had spoken these words, He went forth with His disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which He entered, and His disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: An awful thing is death, and very full of terror, but not to those who have learnt the true wisdom which is above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that knows nothing certain concerning things to come, but deems it to be a certain dissolution and end of life, with reason shudders and is afraid, as though he were passing into non-existence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we who, by the grace of God, have learnt the hidden and secret things of His wisdom, and deem the action to be a departure to another place, should have no reason to tremble, but rather to rejoice and be glad, that leaving this perishable life we go to one far better and brighter, and which hath no end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which Christ teaching by His actions, goeth to His Passion, not by constraint and necessity, but willingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things,” it saith, “Jesus spake, and departed ‘beyond the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which He entered, and His disciples.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Judas also, which betrayed Him, knew the place; for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with His disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He journeyeth at midnight, and crosseth a river, and hasteth to come to a place known to the traitor, lessening the labor to those who plotted against Him, and freeing them from all trouble; and showeth to the disciples that He came willingly to the action, (a thing which was most of all sufficient to comfort them,) and placeth Himself in the garden as in a prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things spake Jesus unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely He was speaking with the Father, surely He was praying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then dost thou not say that, ‘having ceased from the prayer,’ He came there?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it was not prayer, but a speech made on account of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the disciples entered into the garden.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had so freed them from fear that they no longer resisted, but entered with Him into the garden.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how came Judas there, or whence had he gained his information when he came?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is evident from this circumstance, that Jesus generally passed the night out of doors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He been in the habit of spending it at home, Judas would not have come to the desert, but to the house, expecting there to find Him asleep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And lest, hearing of a “garden,” thou shouldest think that Jesus hid Himself, it addeth, that “Judas knew the place”; and not simply so, but that He “often resorted thither with His disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ofttimes He was with them apart, conversing on necessary matters, and such as it was not permitted to others to hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He did this especially in mountains and gardens, seeking a place free from disturbance, that their attention might not be distracted from listening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the Chief Priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns, and torches, and weapons.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these men had often at other times sent to seize Him, but had not been able; whence it is plain, that at this time He voluntarily surrendered Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how did they persuade the band?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They were soldiers, who had made it their practice to do anything for money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon Him, went forth, and said, Whom seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, He did not wait to learn this from their coming, but spake and acted without confusion, as knowing all these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But why come they with weapons, when about to seize Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They feared His followers, and for this reason they came upon Him late at night.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They answered Him, Jesus of Nazareth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou His invincible power, how being in the midst of them He disabled their eyes? for that the darkness was not the cause of their not knowing Him, the Evangelist hath shown, by saying, that they had torches also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even had there been no torches, they ought at least to have known Him by His voice; or if they did not know it, how could Judas be ignorant, who had been so continually with Him? for he too stood with them, and knew Him no more than they, but with them fell backward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus did this to show, that not only they could not seize Him, but could not even see Him when in the midst, unless He gave permission.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He saith again, Whom seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What madness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His word threw them backward, yet not even so did they turn, when they had learnt that His power was so great, but again set themselves to the same attempt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He had fulfilled all that was His, then He gave Himself up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He answered, I told you that I Am.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Judas also which betrayed Him stood with them.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See the forbearance of the Evangelist, how he doth not insult over the traitor, but relates what took place, only desiring to prove one thing, that the whole took place with His own consent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, lest any one should say that He Himself brought them to this, by having placed Himself into their hands, and revealed Himself to them; after having shown to them all things which should have been sufficient to repulse them, when they persevered in their wickedness, and had no excuse, He put Himself in their hands, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If therefore ye seek Me, let these go their way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Manifesting until the last hour His lovingkindness towards them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If,” He saith, “ye want Me, have nothing to do with these, for, behold, I give Myself up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That the saying might be fulfilled which He spake, Of those which Thou gavest Me have I lost none.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “loss” He doth not here mean that which is of death, but that which is eternal; though the Evangelist in the present case includes the former also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And one might wonder why they did not seize them with Him, and cut them to pieces, especially when Peter had exasperated them by what he did to the servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who then restrained them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No other than that Power which cast them backward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so the Evangelist, to show that it did not come to pass through their intention, but by the power and decree of Him whom they had seized, has added, “That the saying might be fulfilled which He spake,” that “not one, &c.” ( c. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Peter, therefore, taking courage from His voice, and from what had already happened, arms himself against the assailants, “And how,” saith some one, “doth he who was bidden not to have a scrip, not to have two coats, possess a sword?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Methinks he had prepared it long before, as fearing this very thing which came to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou sayest, “How doth he, who was forbidden even to strike a blow with the hand, become a manslayer?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He certainly had been commanded not to defend himself, but here he did not defend himself, but his Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And besides, they were not as yet perfect or complete.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou desirest to see Peter endued with heavenly wisdom, thou shalt after this behold him wounded, and bearing it meekly, suffering ten thousand dreadful things, and not moved to anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Jesus here also worketh a miracle, both showing that we ought to do good to those who do evil to us, and revealing His own power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore restored the servant’s ear, and said to Peter, that “All they that take the sword shall perish by the sword” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 52 ); and as He did in the case of the basin, when He relaxed his vehemence by a threat, so also here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist adds the name of the servant, because the thing done was very great, not only because He healed him, but because He healed one who had come against Him, and who shortly after would buffet Him, and because He stayed the war which was like to have been kindled from this circumstance against the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this cause the Evangelist hath put the name, so that the men of that time might search and enquire diligently whether these things had really come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not without a cause doth he mention the “right ear,” but as I think desiring to show the impetuosity of the Apostle, that he almost aimed at the head itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Jesus not only restraineth him by a threat, but also calmeth him by other words, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The cup which My Father hath given Me, shall I not drink it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing, that what was done proceeded not from their power, but from His consent, and declaring that He was not one opposed to God but obedient to the Father even unto death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then Jesus was taken; and they bound Him, and led Him away to Annas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why to Annas?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In their pleasure they made a show of what had been done, as though forsooth they had set up a trophy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And he was father-in-law to Caiaphas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now Caiaphas was he which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why doth the Evangelist again remind us of his prophecy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that these things were done for our salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And such is the exceeding force of truth, that even enemies proclaimed these things beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest the listener, hearing of bonds, should be confounded, he reminds him of that prophecy, that the death of Jesus was the salvation of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who is that other disciple?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is the writer himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And wherefore doth he not name himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he lay on the bosom of Jesus, he with reason concealed his name; but now why doth he this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the same reason, for here too he mentions a great good deed, that when all had started away, he followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he conceals himself, and puts Peter before him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was obliged to mention himself, that thou mightest understand that he narrates more exactly than the rest what took place in the hall, as having been himself within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe how he detracts from his own praise; for, lest any one should ask, “How, when all had retreated, did this man enter in farther than Simon?” he saith, that he “was known to the high priest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that no one should wonder that he followed, or cry him up for his manliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the wonder was that matter of Peter, that being in such fear, he came even as far as the hall, when the others had retreated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His coming thither was caused by love, his not entering within by distress and fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Evangelist hath recorded these things, to clear a way for excusing his denial; with regard to himself, he doth not set it down as any great matter that he was known to the high priest, but since he had said that he alone with Jesus went in, lest thou shouldest suppose that the action proceeded from any exalted feelings, he puts also the cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that Peter would have also entered had he been permitted, he shows by the sequel; for when he went out, and bade the damsel who kept the door bring in Peter, he straightway came in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did he not bring him in himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He clung to Christ, and followed Him; on this account he bade the woman bring him in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith the woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art not thou also one of this man’s disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he saith, I am not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sayest thou, Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Didst thou not declare but now, “If need be that I lay down my life for Thee, I will lay it down”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What hath happened then, that thou canst not even endure the questioning of a door-keeper?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it a soldier who questions thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it one of those who seized Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, it is a mean and abject door-keeper, nor is the questioning of a rough kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She saith not, “Art thou a disciple of that cheat and corrupter,” but, “of that man,” which was the expression rather of one pitying and relenting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Peter could not bear any of these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The, “Art not thou also,” is said on this account, that John was within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So mildly did the woman speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he perceived none of this, nor took it into his mind, neither the first time, nor the second, nor the third, but when the cock crew; nor did this even bring him to his senses, till Jesus gave him the bitter look.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he stood warming himself with the servants of the high priest, but Christ was kept bound within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This we say not as accusing Peter, but showing the truth of what had been said by Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The high priest then asked Jesus of His disciples, and of His doctrine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O the wickedness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though he had continually heard Him speaking in the temple and teaching openly, he now desires to be informed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they had no charge to bring, they enquired concerning His disciples, perhaps where they were, and why He had collected them, and with what intention, and on what terms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he said, as desiring to prove Him to be a seditious person and an innovator, since no one gave heed to Him, except them alone, as though His were some factory of wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To overthrow this, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I spake openly to the world, (not to the disciples privately,) I taught openly in the temple.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then, said He nothing in secret?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He did, but not, as they thought, from fear, and to make conspiracies, but if at any time His sayings were too high for the hearing of the many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why askest thou Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ask them which heard Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These are not the words of one speaking arrogantly, but of one confiding in the truth of what He had said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What therefore He said at the beginning, “If I bear witness of Myself, My witness is not true” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31 ), this He now implieth, desiring to render His testimony abundantly credible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Annas mentioned the disciples, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Dost thou ask Me concerning Mine?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ask Mine enemies, ask those who have plotted against Me, who have bound Me; let them speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is an unquestionable proof of truth, when one calls his enemies to be witnesses to what he saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth the high priest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When it would have been right thus to have made the enquiry, that person did not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by smote Him with the palm of his hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What could be more audacious than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shudder, O heaven, be astounded, O earth, at the long-suffering of the Lord, and the senselessness of the servants!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet what was it that He said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Why askest thou Me,” as if refusing to speak, but wishing to remove every pretext for senseless behavior; and being upon this buffeted, though He was able to shake, to annihilate, or to remove all things, He doth not any one of these, but speaketh words able to relax any brutality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He saith, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “If thou canst lay hold on My words, declare it; but if thou canst not, why strikest thou Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the judgment-hall is full of tumult, and trouble, and passion, and confusion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The high priest asked deceitfully and treacherously, Christ answered in a straightforward manner, and as was meet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then was next to be done?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Either to refute, or to accept what He said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This however is not done, but a servant buffets Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So far was this from being a court of justice, and the proceedings those of a conspiracy, and a deed of tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then not having even so made any farther discovery, they send Him bound to Caiaphas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wonderful, by what a lethargy that hot and furious one was possessed, when Jesus was being led away!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After such things as had taken place, he doth not move, but still warms himself, that thou mayest learn how great is the weakness of our nature if God abandoneth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, being questioned, he denies again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then saith “the kinsman of him whose ear Peter cut off, (grieving at what had taken place,) Did I not see thee in the garden?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But neither did the garden bring him to remember what had taken place, nor the great affection which Jesus there had shown by those words, but all these from pressure of anxiety he banished from his mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why have the Evangelists with one accord written concerning him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not as accusing the disciple, but as desiring to teach us, how great an evil it is not to commit all to God, but to trust to one’s self.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou admire the tender care of his Master, who, though a prisoner and bound, took great forethought for His disciple, raising Peter up, when he was down, by His look, and launching him into a sea of tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They lead Him therefore from Caiaphas to Pilate.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was done, in order that the number of His judges might show, even against their will, how fully tested was His truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And it was early.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before cock crow He was brought to Caiaphas, early in the morning to Pilate; whence the Evangelist shows, that being questioned by Caiaphas during an entire half of the night, He was in nothing proved guilty; wherefore Caiaphas sent Him on to Pilate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But leaving these things for the others to relate, John speaks of what follows next.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe the ridiculous conduct of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They who had seized the innocent, and taken up arms, do not enter into the hall of judgment, “lest they should be polluted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And tell me, what kind of pollution was it to set foot in a judgment-hall, where wrong-doers suffer justice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They who paid tithes of mint and anise, did not think they were polluted when bent on killing unjustly, but thought that they polluted themselves by even treading in a court of justice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why did they not kill Him, instead of bringing Him to Pilate?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the first place, the greater part of their rule and authority had been cut away, when their affairs were placed under the power of the Romans; and besides, they feared lest they should afterwards be accused and punished by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what is, ‘That they might eat the Passover?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He had done this on the first day of unleavened bread.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Either he calls the whole feast “the Passover,” or means, that they were then keeping the Passover, while He delivered it to His followers one day sooner, reserving His own Sacrifice for the Preparation-day, when also of old the Passover was celebrated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they, though they had taken up arms, which was unlawful, and were shedding blood, are scrupulous about the place, and bring forth Pilate to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And having gone out, he said, What accusation bring ye against this man?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that he was free from fondness for rule and from malice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For seeing Jesus bound, and led by so many persons, he did not think that they had unquestionable proof of their accusation, but questions them, thinking it a strange thing that they should take for themselves the judgment, and then commit the punishment without any judgment to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O madness! for why do ye not mention His evil deeds, instead of concealing them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do ye not prove the evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that they everywhere avoid a direct accusation, and that they can say nothing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That Annas questioned Him about His doctrine, and having heard Him, sent Him to Caiaphas; and he having in his turn questioned Him, and discovered, nothing, sent Him to Pilate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate saith, “What accusation bring ye against this man?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor here have they anything to say, but again employ certain conjectures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At which Pilate being perplexed saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Take ye him and judge him according to your law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They therefore said, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this they said, “that the saying of the Lord might be fulfilled, which He spake, signifying by what death He should die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how did the expression, ‘It is not lawful for us to put any man to death,’ declare this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Either the Evangelist means that He was about to be slain not by the Jews only, but by the Gentiles also, or that it was not lawful for them to crucify.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if they say, “It is not lawful for us to put any man to death,” they say it with reference to that season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that they did slay men, and that they slew them in a different way, Stephen shows, being stoned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they desired to crucify Him, that they might make a display of the manner of His death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate, wishing to be freed from trouble, doth not dismiss Him for a long trial, but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Having entered in, he asked Jesus, and said, Art thou the King of the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did Christ ask this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He desired to expose the evil intentions of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate had heard this saying from many, and, since the accusers had nothing to say, in order that the enquiry might not be a long one, he desires to bring forward that which was continually reported.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when he said to them, “Judge him according to your law,” wishing to show that His offense was not a Jewish one, they replied, “It is not lawful for us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He hath not sinned against our law, but the indictment is general.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate then, having perceived this, saith, as being (himself) likely to be endangered, “Art thou the King of the Jews?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then Jesus, not from ignorance, but from a desire that the Jews should be accused even by him, asked him, saying, “Did others tell it thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this point then declaring himself, Pilate replied,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Am I a Jew?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me; what hast thou done?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here desiring to clear himself of the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then because he had said, “Art thou the King?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus reproving him answereth, “This thou hast heard from the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou not make accurate enquiry?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They have said that I am a malefactor; ask them what evil I have done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this thou doest not, but art simply framing charges against Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself,” or from others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate then cannot at once say that he had heard it, but simply goes along with the people, saying, “They have delivered thee unto me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I must needs therefore ask thee what thou hast done.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My Kingdom is not of this world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He leadeth upwards Pilate who was not a very wicked man, nor after their fashion, and desireth to show that He is not a mere man, but God and the Son of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If My Kingdom were of this world, then would My servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He undoeth that which Pilate for a while had feared, namely, the suspicion of seizing kingly power, “Is then His kingdom not of this world also?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Certainly it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then saith He it ‘is not’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not because He doth not rule here, but because He hath his empire from above, and because it is not human, but far greater than this and more splendid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If then it be greater, how was He made captive by the other?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By consenting, and giving Himself up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He doth not at present reveal this, but what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I had been of this world, ‘My servants would fight, that I should not be delivered.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He showeth the weakness of kingship among us, that its strength lies in servants; but that which is above is sufficient for itself, needing nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this the heretics taking occasion say, that He is different from the Creator.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then, when it saith, “He came to His own”? ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What, when Himself saith, “They are not of this world, as I am not of this world”? ( c. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also He saith that His kingdom is not from hence, not depriving the world of His providence and superintendence, but showing, as I said, that His power was not human or perishable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then said Pilate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou a king then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a King.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this end was I born.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then He was born a king, all His other attributes are by Generation, and He hath nothing which He received in addition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that when thou hearest that, “As the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son also to have life” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ), deem of nothing else but His generation, and so of the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And for this cause came I, that I should bear witness unto the truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “that I should speak this very thing, and teach it, and persuade all men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou, O man, when thou hearest these things, and seest thy Lord bound and led about, deem present things to be nought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how can it be otherwise than strange, if Christ bore such things for thy sake, and thou often canst not endure even words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He is spit upon, and dost thou deck thyself with garments and rings, and, if thou gain not good report from all, think life unbearable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He is insulted, beareth mockings, and scornful blows upon the cheek; and dost thou wish everywhere to be honored, and bearest thou not the reproaching of Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hearest thou not Paul saying, “Be ye imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ”? ( 1 Cor. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore any one makes a jest of thee, remember thy Lord, that in mockery they bowed the knee before Him, and worried Him both by words and deeds, and treated Him with much irony; but He not only did not defend Himself, but even repaid them with the contraries, with mildness and gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Him now let us emulate; so shall we be enabled even to be delivered from all insult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is not the insulter that gives effect to acts of insult, and makes them biting, but he who is little of soul, and is pained by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou art not pained, thou hast not been insulted; for the suffering from injuries depends not on those who inflict, but on those who undergo them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou grieve at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If a man hath insulted thee unjustly, in this case surely thou oughtest not to grieve at all, but to pity him; if justly, much more oughtest thou to keep quiet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For should any one address thee, a poor man, as though thou wert rich, the praise contained in his words is nothing to thee, but his encomium is rather mockery; and so if one insulting thee utter things that are untrue, the reproach is nothing to thee either.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if conscience takes hold of what hath been said, be not grieved at the words, but make correction in deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This I say with regard to what really are insults.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if one reproach thee with poverty or low birth, laugh at him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things are a reproach not to the hearer, but to the speaker, as not knowing true wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “when these things are said in the presence of many who are ignorant of the truth, the wound becomes unbearable.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, it is most bearable, when you have an audience present of witnesses praising and applauding you, scoffing at and making a jest of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For not he that defends himself, but he that saith nothing, is applauded by sensible persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if none of those present be a sensible person, then laugh at him most of all, and delight thyself in the audience of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there all will praise and applaud and welcome thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one Angel is as good as all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of Angels, when the Lord Himself proclaimeth thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us exercise ourselves with these reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is no loss to be silent when insulted, but it is, on the contrary, to defend one’s self when insulted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since were it a fault silently to bear what is said, Christ would never have told us, “If one smite thee on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then our enemy say what is not true, let us on this account even pity him, because he draws down upon him the punishment and vengeance of the accusers, being unworthy even to read the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to the sinner God saith, “Why declarest thou My statutes, and takest My covenant in thy mouth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou satest and spakest against thy brother.” ( Ps. l.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 and 20 , LXX.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if he speak the truth, so also he is to be pitied; since even the Pharisee spake the truth; yet he did no harm to him who heard him, but rather good, while he deprived himself of ten thousand blessings, enduring shipwreck by this accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that either way it is he that suffers injury, not thou; but thou, if thou art sober, wilt have double gain; both the propitiating God by thy silence, and the becoming yet more discreet, the gaining an opportunity from what hath been said to correct what has been done, and the despising mortal glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the source of our pain, that many gape upon the opinion of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we are minded to be thus truly wise, we shall know well that human things are nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us learn then, and having reckoned up our faults, let us accomplish their correction in time, and let us determine to correct one this month, another next month, and a third in that which follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so mounting as it were by steps, let us get to heaven by a Jacob’s ladder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the ladder seems to me to signify in a riddle by that vision the gradual ascent by means of virtue, by which it is possible for us to ascend from earth to heaven, not using material steps, but improvement and correction of manners.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then lay hold on this means of departure and ascent, that having obtained heaven, we may also enjoy all the blessings there, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever.
